headline: David Schulhof, MUSC ETF CEO: Fortt Knox Innovation Lab
summary: David Schulhof, who sold EverGreen to KKR, launched MUSQ on NYSE as the first pure-play music ETF; it bundles UMG, Warner, Sony, Spotify and Live Nation. Public music equities trade at 10-15x EBITDA versus 20-25x private, he argues, citing Ackman's $64B UMG bid and Concord-BMG's $15B fourth-major deal. At his Amplify Music Investment Summit, he said he wants Congress to extend Tennessee's ELVIS Act to stop Suno, OpenAI and Grok cloning Drake or The Weeknd.

Public music equities trade at 10-15x EBITDA versus 20-25x private, he argues, citing Ackman's $64B UMG bid and Concord-BMG's $15B fourth-major deal. At his Amplify Music Investment Summit, he said he wants Congress to extend Tennessee's ELVIS Act to stop Suno, OpenAI and Grok cloning Drake or The Weeknd.

headline: Larry Miller, NYU Music Business Professor: Fortt Knox Innovation Lab
summary: At the Amplify Music Investment Summit, NYU Professor Larry Miller calls streaming the biggest of a century's music disruptions, with catalogs fetching $1.3 billion-plus after peer-to-peer nearly gutted IP. Spotify won on UX where a sued-out Napster could have. China is now the fourth-largest market via ad tiers. Taylor Swift's clockwork releases and $2 billion-plus Eras Tour contrast Jay-Z's portfolio play across media and culture; Bad Bunny's Spanish Super Bowl drove streaming.
